user.title: Connect to Miniflux
user.description: Enter your Miniflux server address and an API key (create one in Miniflux under Settings → API Keys).

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate Miniflux
reauth_confirm.description: The API key for this Miniflux instance no longer works. Enter a new one.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the Miniflux server. Check the URL and that it is reachable.

abort.already_configured: This Miniflux instance and user are already configured.

webhook.title: Webhook

search_entries.name: Search entries
search_entries.description: Query entries with the shared filter contract (category/feed/status/starred/search/date range), paginated internally up to the given limit.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

status.name: Status
status.description: One or more of unread/read/removed. Omit or pass an empty list to match any status.

published_within.name: Published within
published_within.description: Duration window ending now. Cannot be combined with published after/published before.

limit.name: Limit
limit.description: Maximum entries to return (auto-paginated internally up to this count).

include_content.name: Include content
include_content.description: Include full article HTML in the response. Off by default to keep responses small.

count_entries.name: Count entries
count_entries.description: Cheap pre-flight count using the same filter contract as Search entries, without fetching entry bodies.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

status.name: Status
status.description: One or more of unread/read/removed. Omit or pass an empty list to match any status.

published_within.name: Published within
published_within.description: Duration window ending now. Cannot be combined with published after/published before.

get_entries.name: Get entries
get_entries.description: Hydrate specific entries by id -- the counterpart to compact webhook events.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

entry_ids.name: Entry IDs
entry_ids.description: Entry ids to hydrate (e.g. from a miniflux_new_entries event). Ids that no longer exist are returned in `missing`, not treated as an error.

include_content.name: Include content
include_content.description: Include full article HTML. On by default, since hydrating entries usually means you want the body.

get_feeds.name: Get feeds
get_feeds.description: List feeds, optionally filtered by category or narrowed to feeds currently failing to parse.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

only_with_errors.name: Only with errors
only_with_errors.description: Only return feeds with a non-zero parsing error count.

get_categories.name: Get categories
get_categories.description: List every category, including ones with no feeds -- the only way to see an empty category (feed_count/unread are 'as of last poll'; null when the poll snapshot has no data for that category yet).

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

update_entries.name: Update entries
update_entries.description: Declarative status/starred update over an explicit list of entry ids -- never a scope. At least one of Status/Starred is required.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

entry_ids.name: Entry IDs
entry_ids.description: Exactly these entries are affected -- never a scope.

starred.name: Starred
starred.description: Declarative -- entries already in the target state are left untouched (idempotent, safe to retry).

mark_all_read.name: Mark all read
mark_all_read.description: Marks an entire scope as read. Prefer Update entries with an explicit id list for pipeline automations -- entries that arrive between a query and this call would be silently marked read without ever being processed. This service is for the human "declare inbox bankruptcy" case.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

feed.name: Feed
feed.description: Exactly one of Feed/Category/Everything is required.

everything.name: Everything
everything.description: Mark every entry across the whole instance as read.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

crawler.name: Crawler
crawler.description: Use the headless-browser crawler for this feed instead of a plain HTTP fetch.

update_feed.name: Update feed
update_feed.description: Update one or more mutable fields on an existing feed.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

crawler.name: Crawler
crawler.description: Use the headless-browser crawler for this feed instead of a plain HTTP fetch.

delete_feed.name: Delete feed
delete_feed.description: Permanently deletes a feed and its entries from Miniflux. Not reversible.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

refresh_feed.name: Refresh feed
refresh_feed.description: Fetch a single feed now instead of waiting for its schedule.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

refresh_all_feeds.name: Refresh all feeds
refresh_all_feeds.description: Refreshes every feed on the instance. Separate from Refresh feed on purpose -- the two can never be confused by a missing argument.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

discover_feeds.name: Discover feeds
discover_feeds.description: Probes a site URL for candidate RSS/Atom feeds, without subscribing to any of them.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

delete_category.name: Delete category
delete_category.description: Deletes a category AND every feed inside it, along with those feeds' entries. Not reversible -- the most destructive single call in this integration.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

export_opml.name: Export OPML
export_opml.description: Returns the instance's full feed list as an OPML string. Useful for a scheduled backup automation.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

import_opml.name: Import OPML
import_opml.description: Import feeds and categories from an OPML document.

config_entry_id.name: Miniflux instance
config_entry_id.description: Target a specific Miniflux instance. Required only if more than one is configured.

webhook_secret_missing.title: Miniflux webhook secret is not configured
webhook_secret_missing.description: The webhook endpoint for {instance_url} is live, but no secret is configured, so every delivery is being rejected with HTTP 401. Open this integration's Configure → Webhook step, paste the secret Miniflux generated when you saved the webhook URL there, and save.

webhook_signature_failing.title: Miniflux webhook deliveries are failing verification
webhook_signature_failing.description: Several deliveries in a row from {instance_url} failed signature verification. This usually means the secret was regenerated (for example, the webhook URL was re-saved in Miniflux) and no longer matches what's configured here. Open this integration's Configure → Webhook step and re-paste the current secret from Miniflux.
